from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of ``Triple`` objects into a fluent, grammatically
    correct sentence.
    All triples sharing the same subject are combined into a single clause;
    different subjects generate separate sentences.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to human‑readable phrase generators.
    phrase_map = {
        # Airport‑specific
        "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda o: f"is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda o: f"has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway number {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda o: f"has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a first runway number {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has ICAO code {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has IATA code {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda o: f"is also known as {o}",
        # Geopolitical
        "leaderParty": lambda o: f"has a leader from the {o} party",
        "capital": lambda o: f"has capital {o}",
        "language": lambda o: f"speaks {o.replace(' languages','').replace(' language','')}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"official language is {o}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
        "mayor": lambda o: f"has mayor {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda o: f"has largest city {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda o: f"has county seat {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda o: f"is in the administrative arrondissement {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is in the city of {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda o: f"is under jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"people are called {o}",
        # Military / organization
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda o: f"operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": lambda o: f"operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda o: f"operates fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": lambda o: f"operates attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": lambda o: f"participated in the {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda o: f"has hub airport {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda o: f"serves region {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has leader title {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f"has headquarters at {o}",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses currency {o}",
        # Taxonomy
        "class": lambda o: f"is classified as {o}",
        "division": lambda o: f"belongs to division {o}",
        "order": lambda o: f"belongs to order {o}",
        # Miscellaneous
        "foundingYear": lambda o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda o: f"has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda o: f"has area code {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda o: f"is in ceremonial county {o}",
        "chief": lambda o: f"is led by chief {o}",
    }

    from collections import defaultdict

    # Group triples by subject and predicate to combine multiple objects
    subj_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    for t in triples:
        subj_pred_objs[t.subject][t.predicate].append(t.object)

    # Build phrases for each subject
    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subj_pred_objs.items():
        phrases: list[str] = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            # Remove duplicate objects while preserving order
            seen = set()
            unique_objs = [x for x in objs if not (x in seen or seen.add(x))]
            generator = phrase_map.get(pred)
            if generator:
                if len(unique_objs) == 1:
                    phrase = generator(unique_objs[0])
                else:
                    combined_obj = " and ".join(unique_objs)
                    phrase = generator(combined_obj)
            else:
                phrase = f"{pred} {' and '.join(unique_objs)}"
            phrases.append(phrase)
        subject_phrases[subj] = phrases

    # Build clauses with proper Oxford comma
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clauses.append(f"{subj} {phrases[0]}")
        else:
            clauses.append(f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1])

    # Combine all subjects into one complex sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(clauses) + "."

    # Capitalise the first character.
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

    # Build a readable sentence by processing each triple in order.

    parts: list[str] = []

        if generator:
            phrase = generator(t.object)
            parts.append(f"{t.subject} {phrase}")
        else:
            parts.append(f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}")

    # Combine parts into a single complex sentence.
    if not parts:
        return ""
    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0] + "."
    elif len(parts) == 2:
        sentence = parts[0] + ", and " + parts[1] + "."
    else:
        # Use semicolons for intermediate clauses and a final "and"
        sentence = parts[0]
        for i, part in enumerate(parts[1:], 1):
            if i == len(parts) - 1:

            else:
                sentence += "; " + part
        sentence += "."

                sentence += ", and " + part
            else:
                sentence += "; " + part
        sentence += "."

    # Capitalise the first character.
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    from collections import defaultdict
    
    # Group triples by subject and predicate to combine multiple objects
    subj_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    for t in triples:
        subj_pred_objs[t.subject][t.predicate].append(t.object)
    
    # Build phrases for each subject
    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subj_pred_objs.items():
        phrases: list[str] = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            # Remove duplicate objects while preserving order
            seen = set()
            unique_objs = [x for x in objs if not (x in seen or seen.add(x))]
            generator = phrase_map.get(pred)
            if generator:
                if len(unique_objs) == 1:
                    phrase = generator(unique_objs[0])
                else:
                    combined_obj = " and ".join(unique_objs)
                    phrase = generator(combined_obj)
            else:
                phrase = f"{pred} {' and '.join(unique_objs)}"
            phrases.append(phrase)
        subject_phrases[subj] = phrases
    
    # Build clauses with proper Oxford comma
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clauses.append(f"{subj} {phrases[0]}")
        else:
            clauses.append(f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1])
    
    # Combine all subjects into one complex sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(clauses) + "."
    
    # Capitalise the first character.
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
